23.reason: Error: Should print the hyphen character '-' without quotes. Using "'-'" prints the literal string '-', not the hyphen character.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 1.0 , reason:  Error: Should print the hyphen character '-' without quotes. Using "'-'" prints the literal string '-', not the hyphen character.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 1.0 , reason:  Error: Should print the hyphen character '-' directly, not the string "'-'". This will output the literal characters ' - ' instead of just '-'.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.25 , reason:  Should print '-' without quotes to match required output.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 1.0 , reason:  [Should print just the dash character '-' without quotes. Correct: printf("-");]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  [The single quotes are part of the string literal and will be printed literally, resulting in incorrect output. It should be printf("-"); to output only the hyphen character.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  [Error: prints literal single quotes around '-', should print "-" to output the original character unchanged as per requirements]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 1.0 , reason:  [The problem requires outputting the original '-' character, not the string "'-'". This causes incorrect output for unexpandable cases.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 1.0 , reason:  [The problem requires outputting the '-' character as-is, not the string "'-'". This causes incorrect output for unexpanded cases.]

7.reason: Out-of-bounds access when s[i] is '-' at the end of string; missing check i+1 < strlen(s).

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.25 , reason:  Out-of-bounds access when s[i] is '-' at the end of string; missing check i+1 < strlen(s).
